reasoning: The type `ℕ →ᵇ I` with `I = Icc 0 1` is canonically $[0,1]^\omega$: since ℕ has the discrete topology every function is continuous, and since the codomain [0,1] is itself bounded every function is automatically bounded, so `ℕ →ᵇ I` is in bijection (as a set) with functions ℕ → [0,1]. Mathlib endows `α →ᵇ β` with the uniform metric `sInf {C | 0 ≤ C ∧ ∀ x, dist (f x) (g x) ≤ C}`, which on this space is exactly the uniform (sup) metric topology Munkres refers to. `¬ LocallyCompactSpace (ℕ →ᵇ I)` is the standard negation of local compactness. So the Lean statement asserts precisely that $[0,1]^\omega$ with the uniform topology is not locally compact.
